name: Golf Upgrades Indoor Training Studio
address: 11689 Sorrento Valley Rd L, San Diego, CA 92121
about: Golf Upgrades Indoor Training Studio is a premier year-round golf training facility in San Diego that combines advanced technology with professional coaching. The studio is equipped with Foresight Sports Simulators and a dedicated gym to provide comprehensive training experiences. It offers data-driven swing analysis, ball flight tracking, and personalized instruction for golfers of all skill levels, focusing on both technical skill development and physical fitness. Pricing information is available through detailed lesson and package rates, making it suitable for those seeking structured improvement in a controlled environment.
why_golfers_like_it: Combines advanced Foresight Sports simulators with professional PGA-inspired coaching
Includes a dedicated gym facility for physical fitness and swing improvement
Offers year-round, climate-controlled training regardless of weather conditions
Provides instant swing metrics and ball flight data for data-driven improvement

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of food services on-site
No mention of alcohol services on-site; BYOB not indicated

simulator_hardware.notes: Premium launch monitors; no specific model mentioned

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website doesn't explicitly state if club rentals are available, but it mentions a golf fitting service, suggesting equipment options may be available.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: There is no mention of food or drink services, and no BYOB policy is indicated, so it's best to assume outside food and drinks are not permitted.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website doesn't specify typical round times, but simulator sessions are typically booked by the hour rather than by the number of holes.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Golf Upgrades offers junior programs including PGA Junior League and summer camps 2025, indicating they cater to younger golfers.
